title: rubus hirtus
intro: Rubus hirtus, also known as the hairy raspberry or wood raspberry, is a perennial plant species in the rose family, characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Asia, where its roots and leaves are valued for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus are attributed to its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or powder, making it a versatile ingredient in traditional medicine and modern herbal remedies.
